description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a wooden-textured fuselage and dual stacked wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft. The wings are straight and braced with visible struts and wires, featuring roundels on the upper surfaces. The nose houses a single propeller and a metallic engine cowling, while the undercarriage consists of two fixed wheels. The tail section is painted with a tricolor pattern and includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windscreen and a visible pilot figure.
description: A classic wooden biplane with dual wings, open cockpit, and a single front propeller, featuring roundels and a tricolor tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden fuselage, dual wings, open cockpit, and tricolor tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual stacked wings, roundels, propeller, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the cockpit, engine cowling, and tail design.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, tricolor rudder, and the structure of the wings and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts and wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers; tricolor rudder.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage with a small windscreen.
